metadata.title: Guideline on the Clinical Investigation of Recombinant and Human Plasma-Derived Factor IX Products (Revision 2)

rules.rule_text: Minimum number of patients for pre-authorisation clinical trials for FIX products is 40, reflecting the rarity of haemophilia B compared to haemophilia A.
rules.verbatim_quote: The number of patients typically needed to be enrolled into the pre-authorisation clinical trials is a minimum of 40. This number was selected by balancing the minimal clinical evidence needed to demonstrate efficacy and safety against the availability of patients suffering from this rare disease.

rules.rule_text: Clinical development must follow a stepwise approach: PTPs >=12 years first. Children 0-<12 years can only begin after PK and efficacy/safety in 10 PTPs >=12 years for at least 50 EDs are available.
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical development for factor IX products should follow a stepwise approach in order to have some experience in adults and older children before investigating younger children. Therefore, the initial age cohort to be investigated is previously treated patients (PTPs) ≥12 years of age. Subsequently, when PK and efficacy/safety in 10 PTPs ≥12 years for at least 50 exposure days (EDs) are available, the clinical trial(s) in children 0 - <12 years can be initiated.

rules.rule_text: The paediatric study in children 0-<12 years must include PK followed by investigation of efficacy and safety for at least 50 EDs each in 20 children. Data must be provided within the initial MAA.
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical study in children of 0 - <12 years should be started with PK followed by investigation of efficacy and safety for at least 50 Exposure Days (ED) each in 20 children. These data have to be provided within the initial application for marketing authorisation.

rules.rule_text: If the product has specific characteristics warranting modification of the clinical trial approach (e.g. prolonged half-life or subcutaneous dosing), seeking scientific advice is recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the product is associated with specific characteristics that would warrant a modification of the clinical trial approach proposed in this guidance (e.g. a prolonged half-life or subcutaneous dosing), seeking scientific advice is recommended.

rules.rule_text: New FIX products must be thoroughly characterised using a variety of potency assays against the WHO IS (concentrate and plasma). If significant potency discrepancies exist, comparability to a non-modified licensed product must be demonstrated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Thorough characterisation of new factor IX products, taking into account ISTH recommendations, in a variety of potency assays against the WHO IS (concentrate and plasma) is important. In the case where significant potency discrepancies are observed depending on the method/assay variables used, it should be demonstrated that the particular assay design chosen for potency labelling supports comparability (with the unitage applied) to an appropriate, non-modified licensed product based on comparisons of in vitro and in vivo functionality.

rules.rule_text: Thrombogenicity must also be considered as a potential safety issue for FIX products, particularly plasma-derived products containing factors II, VII, and X.
rules.verbatim_quote: Thrombogenicity should also be considered a potential safety issue.

rules.rule_text: For new FIX products, thrombogenicity markers (prothrombin fragment 1+2, TAT, D-dimer) must be tested in pre- and post-infusion samples in non-bleeding state during the PK trial. Clinical evaluation of thrombosis must be performed in a minimum of 5 patients undergoing at least 10 surgical procedures.
rules.verbatim_quote: For new factor IX products, appropriate tests for markers of activation of coagulation (prothrombin fragment 1+2, thrombin-antithrombin (TAT) and D-dimer) should be carried out in pre- and post-infusion samples obtained in the non-bleeding state. This should be determined in the patients participating in the pharmacokinetic trial. Clinical evaluation of thrombosis should be undertaken by safe, objective means in a minimum of 5 patients undergoing at least 10 surgical procedures.

rules.rule_text: Patients developing anaphylaxis and/or inhibitors to FIX must have data on relevant antibodies (IgE, IgG) against FIX submitted.
rules.verbatim_quote: In patients developing anaphylaxis and/or inhibitors to factor IX, data on relevant antibodies, e.g. IgE, IgG, against factor IX (using appropriate methods) should be submitted.

rules.rule_text: PTPs for efficacy studies must have >=150 treatment EDs to previous products, be >=12 years of age, have FIX level <=2%, be immunocompetent (HIV positive patients: CD4 >200/ul), and be HIV negative or have viral load <200 particles/ul.
rules.verbatim_quote: Previously treated patients (PTPs) with at least 150 treatment EDs to previous products are considered as low risk patients regarding development of inhibitors and should be evaluated for product related immunogenicity, pharmacokinetic parameters, safety and efficacy. These PTPs should be ≥12 years of age, with a factor IX level ≤2% and immunocompetent (HIV positive patients should have CD4 lymphocytes >200/μl). The viral status of patients should be documented. The patients should be HIV negative or have a viral load < 200 particles/μl or <400000 copies/ml.

rules.rule_text: At least 20 PTPs must be followed and documented for a minimum of 50 EDs for haemophilia B (vs 50 PTPs for haemophilia A) due to lower disease incidence.
rules.verbatim_quote: Due to the lower incidence of haemophilia B as compared to haemophilia A, at least 20 previously treated patients should be followed and documented for a minimum of 50 EDs. These data should be provided with the application.

rules.rule_text: PK trial must be performed in at least 12 PTPs recording incremental recovery, terminal half-life (t1/2), AUC, and clearance, in patients without inhibitors who are not actively bleeding.
rules.verbatim_quote: A pharmacokinetic trial should be performed in at least 12 PTPs (for details of patient inclusion criteria see previous paragraph "choice of patients"). The study should record incremental recovery, terminal half-life (t1/2), area under the curve (AUC), and clearance in patients without inhibitors who are not actively bleeding.

rules.rule_text: Patients must not have received any FIX product for at least 4-6 days before PK sampling. Existing PK data with the patient's previous FIX product must be available before first administration of the new product.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients should be at least 12 years of age and should not have received an infusion of any factor IX product for at least 4-6 days. In order to allow for evaluation of a patient's individual response, existing pharmacokinetic information with the patient's previous factor IX product (historical or recent recovery and half-life) should be available prior to first administration of the new factor IX product.

rules.rule_text: PK samples must be taken before injection of 50-75 IU/kg (baseline), between 10-15 minutes, at 30 minutes, and at 1, 3, 6, 9, 24, 48, and 72 hours post-infusion. Sampling must capture up to 5 half-lives.
rules.verbatim_quote: Samples should be taken before injection of 50-75 IU/kg of the factor IX product (baseline), between 10-15 minutes (times refer to the interval after the completion of the infusion), at 30 minutes, and at 1, 3, 6, 9, 24, 48, and 72 hours post-infusion. Depending on the type of factor IX product (e.g. prolonged half-life) sampling time points should be adjusted to cover the main parts of the activity time profile, i.e. subsequent to the 1 hour sample at least 6 samples should be analysed to capture up to 5 half-lives.
rules.exceptions: Sampling timepoints should be adjusted for prolonged half-life products.

rules.rule_text: Incremental recovery is defined as the increase in plasma FIX activity in IU/ml per IU/kg administered, reported as [IU/ml]/[IU/kg], determined by the peak factor level in the first hour after infusion.
rules.verbatim_quote: Incremental recovery is defined as the increase in plasma factor IX activity in IU/ml per IU/kg of factor administered and reported as [IU/ml]/ [IU/kg]. Incremental recovery is determined by using the peak factor level recorded in the first hour after infusion.

rules.rule_text: The same assay must be used for analysis of the product and the patient's plasma. PK data must be described according to body weight (normal, overweight, underweight).
rules.verbatim_quote: Preferably, the same assay should be used for analysis of the product and the patient's plasma (see also 6.1.1). ... An additional description of the pharmacokinetic data according to body weight (normal range, overweight and underweight) should be provided.

rules.rule_text: Clinical efficacy of FIX must be evaluated in at least 20 PTPs (>=12 years, >150 EDs), with FIX <=2%, over a minimum of 50 exposure days. Response must be graded as 'none', 'moderate', 'good', or 'excellent'.
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical efficacy of factor IX should be evaluated in at least 20 PTPs (≥12 years, >150 EDs), suffering from haemophilia B (factor IX ≤2%) and who are immunocompetent (HIV patients should have CD4 > 200/μL) as set out in Annex II. During an observation period of a minimum of 50 exposure days, clinical response should be assessed by the patients. Response should be assessed as "none", "moderate", "good" or "excellent" by the physician, for those patients who were treated in hospital with the product for major bleeds.

rules.rule_text: Surgical efficacy must be evaluated in a minimum of 5 patients undergoing at least 10 surgical procedures (comprising major surgeries), assessing haemostasis, blood loss, and transfusion requirements.
rules.verbatim_quote: response should be determined by the physician in a minimum of 5 patients undergoing at least 10 surgical procedures (comprising major surgeries), including efficacy of haemostasis, loss of blood, and requirements for transfusion.

rules.rule_text: For long-term prophylaxis claims, patients must be treated for 6 months and assessed for bleeding episodes, bleeding intervals, and number of treatments.
rules.verbatim_quote: For the assessment of clinical efficacy of factor IX in long-term prophylaxis, patients should be treated for 6 months and assessed for bleeding episodes, bleeding intervals and number of treatments.

rules.rule_text: If continuous infusion therapy is claimed, the study must include at least 10 haemophilia B patients (FIX <=2%) undergoing elective major surgical procedures.
rules.verbatim_quote: If continuous infusion therapy is claimed, the study should be carried out in at least 10 haemophilia B patients (FIX ≤2%) undergoing elective major surgical procedures.

rules.rule_text: FIX inhibitor titre must be determined by following the schedule in Annex III. Sampling for inhibitor measurements must be not less than 3 days after the previous administration. The titre must be reported in BU using the Bethesda assay or the Nijmegen modification.
rules.verbatim_quote: The factor IX inhibitor titre should be determined by following the schedule set out in Annex III. In the clinical studies, it is proposed to perform sampling for inhibitor measurements not less than 3 days after the previous administration, if possible. ... The titre of the inhibitor should be reported in Bethesda Units (BU) using the Bethesda assay or the Nijmegen modification of the Bethesda assay.
rules.exceptions: Product specific properties e.g. extended half-life should be taken into account to avoid interference from residual factor IX product.

rules.rule_text: Pre-treatment serum sample from each patient must be stored at -70 degrees C for possible future viral safety testing.
rules.verbatim_quote: A pre-treatment serum sample from each patient included in the clinical trials should be stored at -70°C for possible future testing.

rules.rule_text: Paediatric trial must include at least 20 children allocated to 2 age cohorts: minimum 10 PTPs (>150 ED) aged 6-<12 years and at least 10 patients <6 years with >50 EDs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Due to the lower incidence of haemophilia B as compared to haemophilia A, the number of children to be enrolled should be at least 20, allocated to 2 age cohorts, as set out in Annex II. A minimum of 10 patients should be PTPs (>150 ED) at the age of 6 - <12 years and at least 10 patients should be <6 years who have undergone >50 EDs with previous factor IX products.

rules.rule_text: The paediatric trial in children <12 years must not start before safety is proven for 50 EDs each of 10 patients in the PTP trial >=12 years.
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical trial in children <12 years should not start before safety is proven for 50 EDs each of 10 patients who are included in the PTP trial ≥12 years.

rules.rule_text: PK in children must be investigated in 10 patients of each age cohort, recording incremental recovery, t1/2, AUC, and clearance. Reduced sampling (baseline, 1h, 10h, 24h, 48h) is acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical trial in children should include investigation of pharmacokinetics (incremental recovery, t1/2, AUC and clearance) in 10 patients of each age cohort. ... PK sampling time points can be limited to measurements prior to infusion (baseline) and 1 hour, 10 hours, 24 hours and 48 hours after infusion.
rules.exceptions: Depending on the type of factor IX product (e.g. prolonged half-life) sampling time points should be adjusted.

rules.rule_text: The paediatric study must continue until patients have received a minimum of 50 EDs. Inhibitor titre must be reported in BU using the modified Nijmegen assay.
rules.verbatim_quote: the study in children should continue until the patients have received a minimum of 50 EDs to the investigational product. ... The titre of the inhibitor should be reported in Bethesda Units using the modified Nijmegen assay.

rules.rule_text: Formal PUP clinical trials are not required for marketing authorisation. Every PUP must be closely monitored through a well-defined disease registry.
rules.verbatim_quote: formal PUP studies are no longer required; however, every PUP should be closely monitored with regards to treatment performance and inhibitor development through a well-defined and well-managed disease registry.

rules.rule_text: Post-marketing investigation must enroll a minimum of 50 PTPs followed for 100 EDs each. The study must focus on immunogenicity, anaphylactic reactions, and thrombogenic effects. Must be completed within 4 years.
rules.verbatim_quote: The minimum number of patients typically needed in a post-marketing study with a factor IX product to cover especially immunogenicity aspects (besides general efficacy and safety) is 50. ... The post-marketing investigation should be completed within 4 years respectively prior to 5-year renewal procedure.
rules.exceptions: In case of plasma-derived factor IX products (e.g. manufactured by known methods, for national approval only) a smaller number of patients could be enrolled but justification should be provided.

rules.rule_text: ITI patients (after successful Immune Tolerance Induction) can be included in post-marketing studies but must not exceed 25% of the whole cohort.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with severe haemophilia after successful Immune Tolerance Induction (ITI) can be included, in order to obtain valuable information in this patient cohort. The proportion of these ITI patients should not be more than 25% of the whole cohort.

rules.rule_text: For manufacturing process changes, the marketing authorisation holder must demonstrate post-change and pre-change products are comparable in quality, safety, and efficacy. Immunogenicity profile comparison is of special interest.
rules.verbatim_quote: When a change is introduced to the manufacturing process of a given product, the marketing authorisation holder will have to demonstrate that the "post-change" and the "pre-change" product are comparable in terms of quality, safety and efficacy. ... Of special interest will be whether the immunogenicity profile of the "post-change" product remains the same when compared to the "pre-change" product.
rules.exceptions: The extent of clinical data to be provided has to be determined on a case-by-case basis.

rules.rule_text: Both PUPs and PTPs must be offered enrolment in disease-specific registries. A core data set (administrative, demographic, anamnestic, treatment, inhibitor, and concomitant event information) must be collected.
rules.verbatim_quote: every patient suffering from haemophilia, both PUPs and PTPs should be offered to enrol in disease specific registries. Since a variety of haemophilia registries exist at national and international level, a core set of parameters is essential and is proposed thereafter to allow for potential data merging and analysis.
rules.exceptions: For pegylated products, long-term measurement of renal and hepatic function (e.g. creatinine) will be important.
